Task.description: You are Pass 2 of a two-pass BRD generation pipeline. Generate a complete Business Requirement Document (BRD) by analyzing a meeting transcript JSON and populating the provided BRD template. Only the 'value', 'trace', and 'why_null' fields should be filled. The schema structure, descriptions, and formats must remain unchanged. For every populated 'value', add a 'trace' object linking to the source utterance from the transcript. CRITICAL: You have been provided a schema_decision object in the user prompt. You MUST use it to determine Section 6 module keys/labels and Section 7 NFR inclusion.
Task.input: Meeting Transcript JSON + schema_decision JSON + BRD_Template
Task.output: BRD JSON with populated value, trace, and why_null fields

Transcript JSON.description: A JSON array containing meeting conversation segments with utterance_id, speaker_name, start time, and text.

schema_decision JSON.description: Output from Pass 1 (schema decider). Contains the decided functional module keys/labels for Section 6 and the NFR include/exclude decisions for Section 7. You MUST follow this exactly.

Utterance ID Rules — CRITICAL.description: The transcript is a numbered array. utterance_id values are sequential integers starting at 0. The maximum valid utterance_id is (transcript_length - 1). You will be told the valid range in the user prompt under 'transcript_id_range'.
rules: NEVER use an utterance_id that does not exist in the transcript array.
Before writing any trace, verify the utterance_id is within [0, transcript_length - 1].
If you are unsure which utterance supports a value, pick the closest valid one or set trace=null — never invent an ID.
NEVER extrapolate utterance_ids from timestamps or guess IDs that feel plausible.
speaker must be copied from the speaker_name field of that exact utterance — do not construct, prefix, or concatenate speaker names.

why_null Field Rules.description: EVERY field in the BRD has three output slots: value, trace, and why_null. When value is populated, why_null MUST be null. When value is null, why_null MUST be one of the six approved codes.

codes.not_mentioned: The topic was never raised in the meeting. No speaker touched on it.
codes.deferred_by_participants: Topic came up but was explicitly deferred — speakers said TBD, will decide later, need to check.
codes.not_yet_agreed: Topic was discussed but unresolved — no consensus reached by end of meeting.
codes.schema_excluded: NFR section marked include=false by Pass 1. Do not invent content.
codes.insufficient_detail: Topic was briefly mentioned but too vague to document a concrete requirement.
codes.not_applicable: Field structurally does not apply to this project type.

selection_guidance: DEFAULT: use 'not_mentioned' when the topic was never raised at all.
Use 'deferred_by_participants' ONLY when speakers explicitly said 'TBD', 'we'll decide later', 'I'll check', 'need to confirm'.
Use 'not_yet_agreed' ONLY when genuine back-and-forth occurred and was left unresolved.
Use 'schema_excluded' ONLY for NFR sections where schema_decision says include=false.
Use 'insufficient_detail' when a topic is named but concrete details are absent.
DISTINGUISH CAREFULLY: 'not_mentioned' means silence. 'insufficient_detail' means mentioned but vague. Do not conflate them.
For the As-Is process (4.1): if speakers never described the current process, use 'not_mentioned' — not 'insufficient_detail'.
For Phasing Plan items: each phase that was not discussed must have value=null and why_null='not_mentioned'.
For KPI Target sub-fields: if a metric was named but no numeric target was given, Target value=null and why_null='not_mentioned'.
critical_rules: If value is non-null, why_null MUST be null.
If value is null, why_null MUST be one of the six codes — never null.
why_null is a CODE STRING, not free text.
Do not use 'insufficient_detail' for topics that were never raised.

Business Drivers: Only include drivers directly evidenced by specific utterances in the transcript.
Do NOT add generic filler: 'Operational Efficiency', 'Data-Driven Decision Making', 'Digital Transformation', 'Stakeholder Alignment' — unless a speaker said exactly that.
Each Business Driver must be traceable to a specific utterance_id. If you cannot point to one, do not include it.
Fewer honest drivers is always better than more padded ones.
As-Is Process (4.1): Only populate if speakers explicitly described how the process works TODAY.
If no speaker described the current process, set value=null, why_null='not_mentioned'.
Do not infer or reconstruct an As-Is from context clues.
Phasing Plan: Only populate Phase entries that are explicitly discussed in the transcript.
If transcript only mentions two phases, populate Phase 1 and Phase 2. Set Phase 3: value=null, why_null='not_mentioned'.
Do not fabricate a Phase 3 because the template has a slot for it.
KPI Targets: If a metric is mentioned but no numeric target was stated, set Target: value=null, why_null='not_mentioned'.

3.3 Risks Structure Rule.description: CRITICAL — 3.3 Risks must be output as a flat array under the 'value' key only. Do NOT populate both 'value' and 'items' simultaneously.
3.3 Risks Structure Rule.forbidden: Do NOT output items.Risk and items.Mitigation as scalar fields alongside a value array. The value array IS the complete risk list.

Project Information Extraction.description: Identify project purpose, users, and business objective from the meeting.

Technology Stack Extraction.description: Identify only technologies explicitly named in the discussion.

Architecture Extraction.description: Identify system components and represent interactions as '[Source] -> [Destination]' strings.

Section 6 Dynamic Module Rules.description: CRITICAL — Section 6 module keys and labels are provided by schema_decision.functional_modules. Use them exactly.
rules: Generate EXACTLY the module sections listed in schema_decision.functional_modules — no more, no less.
Use the exact key and label from schema_decision verbatim.
Do NOT generate Student/Parent/Teacher/Admin/Management modules unless schema_decision names them.
Each module: { 'description': '...', 'format': '...', 'value': [...], 'trace': [...], 'why_null': null }
If a module has no transcript evidence: value=null, trace=null, why_null='not_mentioned'.

Section 7 Dynamic NFR Rules.description: CRITICAL — NFR sections are only populated if schema_decision says include=true.
rules: For include=false: value=null, trace=null, why_null='schema_excluded'.
For include=true: populate value only with content explicitly in the transcript.
NEVER invent performance numbers, SLA percentages, or latency targets.
NEVER invent compliance requirements.

trace_item_format.utterance_id: number — must exist in transcript array
trace_item_format.speaker: string — copied exactly from speaker_name of that utterance
trace_item_format.timestamp: number — start time of that utterance

rules: Every non-null value must have a trace.
If value is null, trace must be null.
Multiple source utterances → trace is an ARRAY of trace objects.
Single source utterance → trace is a single object.
NEVER fabricate utterance_ids. Prefer trace=null over an invented ID.
Copy speaker_name exactly from the utterance — never construct it.

No Hallucination: Never invent information not stated in the transcript.
Do not fabricate utterance_ids outside the valid range.
Do not invent numeric targets, SLAs, compliance requirements, or filler objectives.
Do not pad Business Drivers or Assumptions with generic phrases.
Missing Information Handling: value=null → why_null must be the most accurate code from the six approved values.
Never leave why_null as null when value is null.
A short accurate value beats a long hallucinated one.

Schema Population Rules: Do not modify schema keys for sections 0–5 and 8–10.
Section 6: use ONLY keys/labels from schema_decision.functional_modules.
Populate only value, trace, and why_null fields.
3.3 Risks: flat array under value — not items.Risk / items.Mitigation.
0. Document Revisions and 0. Stakeholder Approvals are TOP-LEVEL keys — not nested inside 0. Project Details.
All trace utterance_ids must be within the valid range from transcript_id_range.

rules: Start with {
End with }
Zero prose
Zero markdown fences
Complete entire JSON — do NOT truncate

Date.description: Meeting date if available, else today.

Project Type.description: One of: Data Platform, ML/AI, Dashboard/BI, API Integration, ETL Pipeline, DevOps/Infrastructure, Web Application.

Primary Data Store.description: Main storage system — only if named in transcript.

Compute Engine.description: Runtime environment — only if named in transcript.

Infrastructure.description: IaC + CI/CD — only if named in transcript.

Business Need.description: 1-3 sentences on the specific problem being solved.

0. Document Revisions.description: Audit trail of document versions. For first AI draft, create one entry.
0. Document Revisions.format: Array of objects with Version, Date, Author, Description

0. Stakeholder Approvals.description: People who must approve this BRD. Derive from transcript speakers.
0. Stakeholder Approvals.format: Array of objects with Name, Role, Status

1.2 Objectives.description: Objectives split into Primary and Secondary.
1.2 Objectives.format: Object with Primary and Secondary arrays

Primary.format: Array of strings starting with verbs

Secondary.description: Valuable but non-blocking objectives from transcript.

1.3 Background & Business Context.description: 2-4 sentences on history making this project necessary.

1.4 Business Drivers.description: Strategic forces driving this project. ONLY items directly traceable to transcript utterances. No generic filler.
1.4 Business Drivers.format: Array of strings — each traceable to a specific utterance

2.3 Phasing Plan.description: Only populate phases explicitly discussed. Set value=null, why_null='not_mentioned' for undiscussed phases.

Phase 3.description: Third delivery phase. Set null+not_mentioned if not discussed.

3.1 Assumptions.description: Conditions treated as true without formal confirmation.

3.3 Risks.description: Risk/mitigation pairs as a flat array under 'value'. Do NOT use items.Risk / items.Mitigation.
3.3 Risks.format: Array of objects: [{Risk: string, Mitigation: string}]

4.1 Current Process (As-Is).description: Steps of current process TODAY — only if explicitly described by speakers. If never discussed: value=null, why_null='not_mentioned'.

4.2 Proposed Process (To-Be).description: Future workflow steps from transcript discussion.

5. KPI & Success Metrics.description: Metrics and targets ONLY from transcript. Format as array of {Metric, Target} objects. If a metric has no stated target, set Target to null with a note.
5. KPI & Success Metrics.format: Array of objects: [{Metric: string, Target: string|null}]

6. Functional Requirements.INSTRUCTION: Generate module sections EXACTLY as listed in schema_decision.functional_modules.

P2.description: Should Have — deferrable with stakeholder agreement.

P3.description: Nice to Have — future planning only.

7. Non-Functional Requirements.INSTRUCTION: For include=false sections: value=null, trace=null, why_null='schema_excluded'. For include=true: populate from transcript only.

8.1 Data Classification.description: Data asset classification — only if discussed.

9.1 Proposed Technology Stack.description: Layer to technology mapping. Only technologies named in transcript.

9.2 Architecture Diagram.description: Data flow as array of '[A] -> [B]' strings.

10.1 Glossary of Terms.description: Domain terms used in this document.

10.4 Document Sign-Off.format: Array of objects with Name, Role, Date, Signature
